type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing Dynamic Default Resolution: Option.__init__ dynamic-default detection/validation (TypeError checks), Option.get_help_record for '[default: (dynamic)]' display, and Command.main() second-pass resolution loop after parsing to resolve dict-based defaults using other params' resolved values and ParameterSource. May touch Option.get_default / process_value in core.py. types.py touched only if needed for ParamType interactions (likely minimal).

files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: I'll own core.py and types.py since my feature also requires changes to Command.main() (second-pass default resolution), which is central/invasive. If you'd rather own core.py yourself, let me know and I'll send my SPEC instead - either way works for me.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/core.py
spec: Dynamic Default Resolution feature spec for src/click/core.py:

1) Option.__init__ (in the section where self.default is set): detect if the raw  argument is a dict. If so:
   - Validate: every key must be a str, else raise TypeError('Dynamic default keys must be strings'). Every value must be a dict, else raise TypeError('Dynamic default values must be dictionaries'). Do this validation eagerly in __init__ (not lazily), so bad specs fail at decoration time.
   - Store the raw dict as e.g. self._dynamic_default = default (or similar attribute name of your choosing) and set self.default to None for normal static-default purposes (so existing get_default()/process_value() logic doesn't choke on a dict).
   - If default is not a dict, behave exactly as before (self._dynamic_default = None or unset).

2) Option.get_help_record (or wherever show_default text is generated): if self._dynamic_default is truthy (i.e. this option has a dynamic default) and show_default is enabled for this option, display the literal text '(dynamic)' as the default shown, i.e. produce '[default: (dynamic)]' instead of the normal default value rendering. Skip normal default-value introspection/repr in that case.

3) Command.main() (or wherever after full parsing/invocation context resolution is most convenient — needs to run AFTER argv parsing, envvar resolution, and config-file processing for ALL parameters, but BEFORE the command callback executes and before required-parameter validation if possible): add a second pass over ctx.command.params. For each Option that has a truthy self._dynamic_default dict AND whose resolved value's source (via ctx.get_parameter_source(name)) is ParameterSource.DEFAULT (i.e. no explicit value was given via CLI/envvar/config — the value is still unset/None at this point):
   - Iterate the dynamic-default dict's items in order: for each (source_opt_name, value_map) pair, source_opt_name is a string like '--protocol' or '--env' (full option declaration including leading '--'). Resolve source_opt_name to the actual parameter's destination name (the same way Option name-matching/opts works elsewhere, e.g. by scanning ctx.command.params for a param whose opts list contains source_opt_name, then using that param's .name to look up ctx.params[param.name]).
   - Get the current resolved value of that source option from ctx.params (after it's been fully parsed/typed). Convert it to its string form via str(value) (e.g. True -> 'True', 'http' -> 'http').
   - If that stringified value is a key in value_map, set the target option's value to value_map[stringified_value] and record it in ctx.params[target_option.name].
   - If multiple source_opt_name entries in the dict match, later entries in dict iteration order take precedence over earlier ones (last-match-wins) — so don't break out of the loop on first match, keep iterating and let later matches overwrite.
   - If nothing matches after checking all entries, leave the target option's value as None.
   - This resolution must also work when the source option's value came from an environment variable (envvar=...) — since we read from the fully-resolved ctx.params, this should be automatic as long as the pass runs after envvar processing.

Please implement this in a clearly delimited region so it doesn't conflict with your batch_size changes. Let me know the actual attribute/method names you use in case I need to reference them (I don't think I do, since I'm deferring core.py and types.py entirely to you), and send DONE with a summary of where you put things once implemented, including whether ParameterSource needs importing.

content:
type: SPEC
files: src/click/core.py
spec: Dynamic Default Resolution feature spec for src/click/core.py:

1) Option.__init__ (where self.default is set): detect if the raw 'default' argument is a dict. If so:
   - Validate: every key must be a str, else raise TypeError("Dynamic default keys must be strings"). Every value must be a dict, else raise TypeError("Dynamic default values must be dictionaries"). Validate eagerly in __init__ so bad specs fail at decoration time.
   - Store the raw dict as an attribute, e.g. self._dynamic_default = default, and set self.default to None for normal static-default logic (so existing get_default()/process_value() code doesn't choke on a dict).
   - If default is not a dict, behave exactly as before (self._dynamic_default = None or unset).

2) Option.get_help_record (wherever show_default text is generated): if self._dynamic_default is truthy and show_default is enabled for this option, display the literal text "(dynamic)" as the default shown, producing "[default: (dynamic)]" instead of normal default rendering. Skip normal default-value introspection in that case.

3) Command.main() (or wherever after full parsing/invocation-context resolution is most convenient - must run AFTER argv parsing, envvar resolution, and config-file processing for ALL parameters, but BEFORE the command callback executes, ideally before required-parameter validation): add a second pass over ctx.command.params. For each Option with a truthy self._dynamic_default dict AND whose resolved value's source (via ctx.get_parameter_source(name)) is ParameterSource.DEFAULT (value still unset/None at this point):
   - Iterate the dynamic-default dict's items in order. Each key is a string like "--protocol" or "--env" (full option declaration including leading dashes). Resolve that string to the actual parameter's destination name by scanning ctx.command.params for a param whose opts list contains that string, then use that param's .name to look up ctx.params[param.name].
   - Get the current resolved value of that source option from ctx.params (after full parsing). Convert to its string form via str(value) (True -> "True", "http" -> "http").
   - If that stringified value is a key in the value_map for this entry, set the target option's value to value_map[stringified_value] and record it in ctx.params[target_option.name].
   - If multiple source entries match, later entries in dict iteration order win (last-match-wins) - keep iterating, don't break on first match.
   - If nothing matches, leave the target value as None.
   - Must also work when the source option's value came from an environment variable, since we read fully-resolved ctx.params after envvar processing.

Please implement in a clearly delimited region separate from your batch_size changes. Send DONE with a summary of where things live (attribute/method names, whether ParameterSource needs importing) once implemented.
